name: Tumbleweed Destiny
review: Ever since I was but a seedling all I wanted was to tumble in the wind. My own roots disowned me and kicked me out when I came out as a diaspore. I think it was destiny. I roamed and drifted through the American southwest with no real cause or care until aliens took me against my will to mars. There I thought I was stuck and doomed to watch aliens stomp over my old town. I did the only thing I knew how to do.. I tumbled and tumbled some more. Before I knew it, I had amassed enough tumbling strength to defeat the toughest of aliens. I saved the earth and now I tumble around town keeping an ever watchful eye out for more aliens.

name: Tumbleweed Destiny
review:
I'll be up front and honest here. Had the game been any bit more expensive than it is. This would have been a negative review. Treat this as a grey/neutral review if you like.

There's barely a game here. The only thing that's really fun here is getting to full power and being able to fly or combo with the tumbleweed, and it time gates you for as long as it can from getting to that one juicy bit of fun.[spoiler]The secret bonus map is also a huge let down, its huge for no real reason. There's no real secrets in it, and the boss is 10x the health of the normal map, making it take ages to drop if you're the only one not afk, and it largely only drops the same cards as the normal ones just a little stronger, with the exception of two new abilities which really serve no purpose since there's no content past this. I guess you could use them to flex on new players? I don't really see the point personally.[/spoiler]

I wont complain too much more here. Game can still be beat in a day even with the time wasting. It's an OK distraction to break up a routine and thankfully not very expensive.
